title: tephrosia lupinoides
intro: Tephrosia lupinoides, also known as the lupine milkpea or lupine pea, is a perennial plant in the pea family, Fabaceae, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce fever, relieve pain, and combat infections. The health benefits of Tephrosia lupinoides include its ability to treat various conditions such as snake bites, rheumatism, and skin diseases, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve digestive health. Tephrosia lupinoides is commonly prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and it can also be used topically in the form of a paste or ointment to treat skin conditions and wounds.
